markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Extract doc comments, TODOs, safety notes, and operator-facing strings as labeled searchable passages. This MT belongs to CodeIndexingAndNavigation. Index code structure and expose precise backend navigation for agents.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Code navigation must use Handshake-managed parsers/adapters and store spans/citations in PostgreSQL.
Tree-sitter is the first parser family; LSP/SCIP-style precision is a Handshake-owned import/projection path, not an outside service requirement.
scope.expected_behavior: DocCommentTodoExtractor leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: DocCommentTodoExtractor is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 commits 5d6490b0 (compiling foundation) + 79e1a7e4 (runtime proof); 43 lib + 11 integration + 1 nav-API tests green on real PostgreSQL under runtime-full,test-utils. Found+fixed impl-Type/struct-Type entity collision. Pending adversarial code review.

concerns: Doc passage entity_key `doc:{path}#L{line}:{kind}` (docs_todo.rs:67) is line-anchored by design, so any edit that shifts lines orphans the old key and mints a new one - the delegate documents this as intended (stale old key). Acceptable but means doc-passage concept entities accumulate across edits (the old keys are not retired here; retirement depends on a staleness sweep that is not wired for concept entities).
Marker scan find_marker (docs_todo.rs:270) is O(MARKERS x text.len()) per comment with an inner find loop; cheap for comments but unbounded by comment size.

A doc comment that merely MENTIONS a marker word (e.g. '/// see the TODO list policy') is reclassified from DocComment to Todo (record_text_passage at docs_todo.rs:225 short-circuits to marker). This can mislabel legitimate doc text as an actionable TODO. Low severity (searchability), but the kind is presented authoritatively.
evasion_probes: Verified word-boundary marker detection rejects TODOLIST/TODOs-substrings (is_word_byte, docs_todo.rs:293) - delegate test marker_word_boundary_avoids_false_positive confirms.

Verified block-comment state machine terminates: in_block cleared on '*/' (docs_todo.rs:101); a never-closed /* runs to EOF and is then never pushed (block_text dropped) - no infinite loop, no panic, just silent drop of an unterminated block.
byte_span uses offsets.get(...).unwrap_or(text.len()) (docs_todo.rs:314) so out-of-range lines do not panic.
Only DocComment passages produce `documents` edges (engine.rs:408); TODO/SAFETY remain searchable concept entities without edges - matches the contract and avoids polluting the call graph.

evidence: Adversarial code review PASS (ADVERSARIAL_CODE_REVIEWER-20260611-PM); only minor intended-behavior notes.

wp_validator_review_v1.review_scope: Validated only MTs that were READY_FOR_VALIDATION in WP-KERNEL-009; no ACP used.

findings.summary: MT-103 is READY_FOR_VALIDATION but declares MT-097 as a dependency, and MT-097 is still CLAIMED rather than validator-completed.
findings.required_remediation: Do not resubmit MT-103 until MT-097 is READY_FOR_VALIDATION and passes WP Validator review, or update the dependency graph through the packet authority if the dependency is no longer real.

findings.summary: The MT contract says doc comments, TODOs, safety notes, and operator-facing strings; implementation only extracts comments/markers. No string-literal/operator-facing extraction or test coverage was found.
findings.required_remediation: After MT-097 is ready/completed, add operator-facing string literal extraction or explicitly narrow the MT contract. Include tests that prove operator-facing strings are indexed separately from TODO/doc-comment markers.

evidence_basis: Authoritative MT JSON contract and current product tree inspection.
Dependency failures use deterministic packet status/dependency reads only; WIP MT work products were not inspected.
remediation_summary: Do not resubmit MT-103 until MT-097 is READY_FOR_VALIDATION and passes WP Validator review, or update the dependency graph through the packet authority if the dependency is no longer real.

After MT-097 is ready/completed, add operator-facing string literal extraction or explicitly narrow the MT contract. Include tests that prove operator-facing strings are indexed separately from TODO/doc-comment markers.

remediation_v2.fail_verdict: FAIL_V1: blocked on MT-097 (now done) + missing operator-facing string extraction coverage
remediation_v2.remediation: MT-097 dependency resolved (done). Added DocPassageKind::OperatorString + extract_operator_strings AST walk (println!/eprintln!/panic!/log + console.*/alert) indexed as own concept entity, never merged with doc/TODO. Tests operator_strings_are_separate_from_doc_and_todo_passages + mt103_operator_strings_indexed_separately_from_markers green on real PG.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-103 requires extracting doc comments, TODO/safety markers, AND operator-facing strings as labeled, separately-searchable passages persisted as PostgreSQL/EventLedger KnowledgeEntity/KnowledgeSpan authority records. The remediation commit (1206793d) adds real operator-facing string extraction as a distinct passage kind, wired into the engine write path and proven end-to-end against real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL. Both prior FAIL_V1 findings are resolved (the MT-097 dependency is paperwork/now-done; the operator-string extraction gap is now implemented and tested).
findings: docs_todo.rs:235-385 adds extract_operator_strings(): an AST walk over RUST_STRING_SINKS (println!/eprintln!/panic!/log/tracing macros, last path segment matched) and JS/TS console.*/alert/prompt/confirm call_expressions, pulling string-literal descendants only from output sinks. Non-sink string bindings are NOT captured (no false positives) — verified by tests at lines 654-658 (Rust) and 738 (JS).

DocPassageKind::OperatorString (docs_todo.rs:35-57) is a distinct kind with entity_key suffix ':operator_string' (entity_key, lines 80-87), so operator strings can never collide-merge with doc_comment/todo concept keys. Unit test operator_strings_are_separate_from_doc_and_todo_passages (docs_todo.rs:666-719) proves the same file's doc comment, TODO, and println! land in three separate kinds with distinct keys.

engine.rs:484-487 wires both extractors into index_code_source; operator strings flow through the same passage write path (engine.rs:594-655) as their own Concept KnowledgeEntity + Text KnowledgeSpan, with passage_kind recorded in detection_provenance. Doc comments get a Documents edge; TODO/SAFETY/OperatorString remain searchable concept entities without edges (engine.rs:640-653) — matches their non-API claim nature.

Sub-rule 2 (real resource touch) PASS: integration test mt103_operator_strings_indexed_separately_from_markers (tests/knowledge_code_index_tests.rs:923-1025) indexes a file into real PostgreSQL via knowledge_pg(), then reads back list_knowledge_entities_by_kind(Concept) and asserts an operator_string concept exists with a cited span, distinct from doc_comment/todo concepts, with no key collision. knowledge_pg_support.rs:49-64 uses ManagedPostgres::ensure_running (Handshake-native managed PG, port 5544); SKIPs loudly only when PG binaries are genuinely absent and panics on any other startup error — never silently green. No SQLite/mock/in-memory (test file header lines 4-7 declares this contract).

Sub-rule 1 (no deferred-live escape) PASS: grep of docs_todo.rs + engine.rs for todo!()/unimplemented!()/unreachable!()/Unavailable/'not yet wired'/'pending MT-' returned no hits. The literals 'todo'/'unimplemented'/'panic'/'unreachable' at docs_todo.rs:197-217 are sink-NAME match-table strings used to identify macro invocations, not placeholder macro calls on a live path.

Spec alignment (current v02.194 §2.3.13.11, 02-system-architecture.md:2694-2719): passages map to the canonical KnowledgeEntity/KnowledgeSpan/KnowledgeEdge families with durable PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority; receipt emitted as KnowledgeValidationRecorded event (engine.rs:507-530). No markdown-vault-as-authority, no SQLite — consistent with the forbidden list.

Non-blocking aside (carried from adversarial review, not a FAIL): a doc comment that merely MENTIONS a marker word is reclassified DocComment->Todo (record_text_passage short-circuits to find_marker at docs_todo.rs:458); low-severity label noise on searchability, not a behavior or spec violation. Block comments never closed are silently dropped (no panic). entity_key is line-anchored so edits orphan old keys (documented intended behavior).
Paperwork aside (expected, non-blocking): packet cites v02.192 anchors while current is v02.194 — same section numbers resolve; not a code concern.
evidence_checked: grep for placeholder escapes (todo!/unimplemented!/Unavailable/pending MT) — clean

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
